figures.caption: Fig. 4. Shape estimation of the PINN trained on simulated observation dataset and Cosserat rod model for random actuations.
Fig. 6. States estimations of the PINN (dashed lines) and the Cosserat rod model (solid lines - not mentioned in the legends) vs. arc-length for a random actuation input.

Fig. 8. Error distributions at the distal terminal points of the tubes. Left: Euclidean distance error. Right: Euclidean error normalized by tube arclength. Both panels compare PINN and Cosserat estimations.
Fig. 7. Shape estimation of the PINN, the Cosserat rod model, and experimental measurements of the terminal points of the tubes. The PINN was trained using 500 experimental observation data points.
Fig. 9. Runtime of the PINN and Cosserat rod model for 5,000 random actuations.
Fig. 2. Block diagram of the PINN including the structure and training loss terms.
